editableNewDialogue.text: Can you rephrase this to sound more polished but still clear: “Please note that we can proceed once we receive the developer’s approval. In the meantime, kindly coordinate access to the unit so we can assess the affected areas.”

Please be advised that we can proceed once we have received the Developer’s approval. In the interim, we kindly request your assistance in coordinating access to the unit so we may assess the affected areas.
Make it a little warmer and less formal, but not too casual.
Just a quick note—once we have the Developer’s approval, we can move forward. In the meantime, please help us coordinate access to the unit so we can check the affected areas.
Also, can you add a line asking them to reply with a preferred schedule?

Just a quick note—once we have the Developer’s approval, we can move forward. In the meantime, please help us coordinate access to the unit so we can check the affected areas. Kindly reply with your preferred schedule so we can arrange the visit accordingly.
If I want this to sound more neutral, what words should I avoid?

Avoid words that imply urgency, blame, or certainty before confirmation—for example: “immediately,” “fault,” “negligence,” “definitely,” “guarantee,” “final,” and “must.” Neutral alternatives include “please,” “may,” “we recommend,” “subject to,” and “once confirmed.”
Also, for the letter, can you keep it consistent with the earlier ones? It’s for The Rise units again—this time it’s a follow-up on 10T-EW and 11T-EW, and I’ll be sending it out by email today.
Sure—share the draft (or key points), and I’ll align the tone and structure with your earlier notices for The Rise, then format it for email sending.
Before we proceed, I’m not really looking to tweak the tone back and forth. Please just keep everything in a consistently professional, formal style moving forward—no casual or “amiable” versions.
Understood—I'll keep the revisions consistently professional and formal from here on, and I won’t provide casual or amiable rewrites unless you explicitly request them.
Yes, exactly. One formal version only each time.

turns.text: Before we proceed, I’m not really looking to tweak the tone back and forth. Please just keep everything in a consistently professional, formal style moving forward—no casual or “amiable” versions.

editableUpdatedMemories.label: Preferences/Interaction_Preferences
editableUpdatedMemories.reasoning: The prior memory suggests the user prefers precise control over formality level (professional/casual/amiable) through repeated adjustments. However, the user now explicitly states they do not want tone toggling and wants a single consistently formal output going forward, which directly contradicts the earlier preference.
editableUpdatedMemories.value: Prefers a consistently professional/formal style and does not want multiple formality-level options unless explicitly requested.

evidence.text: and I’ll be sending it out by email today.

editableUpdatedMemories.label: Context/Communication_Channel
editableUpdatedMemories.reasoning: The user implies the delivery method by mentioning they will be sending it out by email today.
editableUpdatedMemories.value: User plans to send the correspondence via email.
